questions.question: Explain the basic principle behind Bresenham's Line Algorithm. How does it determine which pixel to plot at each step?
keyPoints: Bresenham's algorithm uses integer arithmetic to determine which pixel is closer to the true line path
It uses a decision parameter that compares the midpoint between two candidate pixels to the actual line
If midpoint is below the line, choose lower pixel; if above, choose upper pixel
This minimizes the error between the chosen pixel and the mathematical line

questions.question: Write the initial decision parameter formula and the two update formulas used in Bresenham's Line Algorithm.
keyPoints: Initial: p = 2Δy - Δx where Δy = y2-y1 and Δx = x2-x1
When pk < 0: pk+1 = pk + 2Δy (choose lower pixel, step horizontal)
When pk ≥ 0: pk+1 = pk + 2Δy - 2Δx (choose upper pixel, step diagonal)

questions.question: List three advantages of Bresenham's Line Algorithm over the DDA algorithm.
keyPoints: Uses only integer arithmetic (no floating-point operations)
Faster execution due to simple integer operations
More accurate - no rounding errors
Easily implementable in hardware
Uses only addition, subtraction, and bit shifting

questions.question: How does Bresenham's algorithm handle lines with slope greater than 1?
keyPoints: For slope > 1, swap the roles of x and y coordinates
Treat y as the major axis and x as the minor axis
Apply the same algorithm with x and y interchanged
This ensures the algorithm iterates along the longer dimension

questions.question: Draw the steps of Bresenham's algorithm to draw a line from (2,2) to (8,6). Show the pixel coordinates plotted.
keyPoints: Calculate Δx = 6, Δy = 4
Initial p = 2(4) - 6 = 2
Plot (2,2), then iterate through x = 3 to 8
Apply decision parameter updates at each step

questions.question: Why is Bresenham's algorithm more suitable for hardware implementation compared to DDA?
keyPoints: Uses only integer arithmetic - no floating-point unit required
Uses simple operations: addition, subtraction, bit shifting
Can be implemented with simple digital logic circuits
No division or multiplication by fractions needed
Faster and cheaper to implement in hardware

questions.question: What is the time complexity of Bresenham's Line Algorithm? Explain with reason.
keyPoints: Time complexity is O(n) where n = |x2-x1| or length of line
The algorithm iterates once for each pixel position along the major axis
Each iteration performs constant-time operations (comparisons, additions)
No recalculation of line equation - only incremental updates

questions.question: Derive the decision parameter formulas for Bresenham's Line Algorithm. Show how the initial parameter and update equations are obtained using the midpoint approach.
keyPoints: Consider the midpoint between two candidate pixels at each step
The line equation: F(x,y) = ax + by + c = 0
For a line from (x1,y1) to (x2,y2) with slope < 1: a = Δy, b = -Δx
At midpoint (xk+1, yk+0.5): evaluate F = a(xk+1) + b(yk+0.5) + c
If F < 0: midpoint is below line, choose lower pixel
If F > 0: midpoint is above line, choose upper pixel
Simplify to get: Initial p = 2Δy - Δx, Updates: pk+1 = pk + 2Δy (if pk<0), pk+1 = pk + 2Δy - 2Δx (if pk≥0)

questions.question: Write a complete algorithm/pseudocode for Bresenham's Line Algorithm and explain each step. Include handling for all octants.
keyPoints: Algorithm should include: Input endpoints (x1,y1) and (x2,y2)
Ensure Δx is positive: if x2 < x1, swap endpoints
Calculate Δx and Δy
Handle slope > 1 by swapping x and y
Initialize decision parameter p = 2Δy - Δx
Plot starting pixel
Loop through all x positions
Update p using appropriate formula based on sign
Handle octant switching for complete coverage

questions.question: Compare Bresenham's Line Algorithm with DDA (Digital Differential Analyzer) under the following headings: (a) Type of arithmetic used (b) Accuracy (c) Speed (d) Hardware implementation suitability
keyPoints: (a) Bresenham uses integer arithmetic; DDA uses floating-point
(b) Bresenham is more accurate with exact pixel selection; DDA has rounding errors
(c) Bresenham is faster due to integer ops; DDA slower due to floating-point
(d) Bresenham easily implemented in hardware; DDA requires FPU
Additional: Bresenham avoids divisions; DDA requires division at each step
Bresenham is the standard for line drawing in practice

questions.question: Using Bresenham's algorithm, trace through and list all pixel coordinates that would be plotted for a line from (0,0) to (10,5). Show the decision parameter values at each step.
keyPoints: Δx = 10, Δy = 5
Initial p = 2(5) - 10 = 0
Continue this pattern...
Explain the alternating pattern due to p values
